raw_text: BTW, I disagree with your point.
expected_behavior: BTW should be tagged as NOUN or PROPN and its lemma should stay 'btw', not decomposed into garbage tokens
<EMAIL> token must be preserved unchanged in lemma_text output after PII protect/restore cycle
follow-up may be split into 'follow', '-', 'up' or kept as one token; lemma should be 'follow-up' or 'follow up', not dropped
possessive 'God\'s' should yield lemma 'god' (not 'god\'s') and be tagged NOUN or PROPN; the clitic '\'s' is a separate PART token
'thou' tagged PRON, 'shalt' lemma should be 'shall' (modal), 'kill' lemma 'kill'; archaic morphology may confuse lemmatizer

raw_text: Jesus Christ was born in Bethlehem.
expected_behavior: Jesus, Christ, and Bethlehem should all be tagged PROPN; lemmas should match lowercased forms 'jesus', 'christ', 'bethlehem'

raw_text: I don't agree with that argument.
expected_behavior: 'don\'t' should be split into 'do' (AUX) + 'not' (PART); lemma of 'agree' is 'agree'
'486DX2' and '66MHz' are alphanumeric tokens; Stanza may assign garbage lemmas or tag them as NOUN/NUM; behavior should not crash

raw_text: IMHO this is the best solution.
expected_behavior: 'IMHO' is internet slang for 'In My Humble Opinion'; Stanza likely tags it as NOUN or PROPN; lemma may be 'imho' or 'IMHO'
'electrolytic' should be tagged ADJ with lemma 'electrolytic'; 'capacitor' should be NOUN with lemma 'capacitor'
'alt.atheism' will likely be tokenized as 'alt', '.', 'atheism' — three separate tokens; the dot may trigger sentence boundary if not handled
'Re:' (reply prefix) should ideally lemmatize to 're' tagged as NOUN or ADV; colon may be a separate PUNCT token

raw_text: The data shows a clear pattern.
expected_behavior: 'data' is ambiguous: Latin plural of 'datum', but often used as uncountable mass noun; Stanza may lemmatize to 'datum' or keep 'data'
'He\'s' should split into 'he' (PRON) + 'be' (AUX); 'computing' lemma should be 'compute' (VERB)
<URL> token must be preserved unchanged in lemma_text after PII protect/restore; must not be corrupted or dropped
'3.14' should be tagged NUM; 'GHz' may be tagged NOUN/PROPN with lemma 'ghz' or 'GHz'; no crash expected

raw_text: AFAIK nobody has solved this yet.
expected_behavior: 'AFAIK' (as far as I know) will likely get an incorrect POS tag (NOUN/PROPN/ADV); lemma may be 'afaik'
'soc.religion.christian' will be tokenized into multiple tokens around the dots; may cause spurious sentence boundaries
'vs.' abbreviation may trick the sentence splitter into a boundary; both 'atheism' and 'theism' should be NOUN with correct lemmas

raw_text: RAM and ROM are types of memory.
expected_behavior: 'RAM' and 'ROM' are all-caps acronyms; Stanza may tag them PROPN or NOUN; lemmas may be 'ram', 'rom' or kept uppercase
